race.name: L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France
race.tagline: 106km of Istanbul's savage climbs deliver Tour de France glory to amateurs—3,000 riders, zero traffic.

vitals.registration: Pro-level organization with traffic-free roads and mechanical support; costs TBD
distance_options: Long: 66.4mi, 5200ft — brutal Beykoz loop with king-of-mountain climbs

climate.description: Istanbul's fall delivers 59-77°F perfection with minimal rain, ideal for hammering climbs without overheating. Low wind risk keeps the loop predictable. Pack light layers for coastal chills.

terrain.surface: Silky asphalt winds past historic pavilions and forests; zero gravel, all road purity. Beykoz's paved perfection shines traffic-free. Smooth enough for speed, grippy for descents.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Tour de France branding catapults this newbie into T2 contention despite modest length and unproven climbs. Istanbul's exotic allure and pro org outweigh elevation uncertainty. Prestige alone demands respect from gravel gods.

logistics.explanation: IST and SAW airports dump you 40km from Beykoz—easy shuttle territory. Traffic-free pro support screams competence. Newbie event but Tour org means zero amateur-hour BS.

length.explanation: 106km loop punches above weight with Istanbul flair but no epic century. Solid for mortals, shy for masochists. Fits amateurs without breaking souls.

technicality.explanation: Paved roads, hairpin descents—nothing shredding tires. King-of-mountain segments add spice sans gravel hell. Beginner-friendly for roadies dreaming big.

elevation.explanation: Estimated 1600m feels punchy for 66 miles; Beykoz climbs bite without Alps brutality. Uncertain data docks points but promises real suffering. Tour-worthy vert on Turkish soil.

climate.explanation: Fall goldilocks: mild temps, dry roads. Coastal whims rare but manageable. Beats sweaty summer slogs elsewhere.

altitude.explanation: Sea-level start, low peaks—no hypoxia drama. Pure leg-burner, not oxygen thief. Keeps focus on pure power.

adventure.explanation: Istanbul's Asia-Europe mashup, pavilion starts, village romps—pure magic. Traffic-free tourism on steroids. Gravel gods weep at the romance.

prestige.explanation: Official Tour de France amateur arm in Turkey's debut—global cred instant. 23rd country joins legends. Bucket-list badge of honor.

race_quality.explanation: Pro mechanicals, closed roads, copper trophies—polished as Paris-Roubaix. First-year jitters possible but Tour DNA delivers.

experience.explanation: Küçüksu grandeur, Tour atmosphere for mortals—immersive AF. Videos prove the hype. Once-in-lifetime Istanbul pedal.

community.explanation: 3K internationals spark vibe but Turkey nascent scene limits depth. Global mix builds fast. Potential cult classic.

field_depth.explanation: Amateurs from 30 nations—no pros, solid midpack battle. Inaugural energy high, depth grows with reps.

value.explanation: Prestige per euro killer if fees reasonable; tourism bonus huge. Pro perks justify spend.

expenses.explanation: Istanbul flights steep, local cheap. Event TBD but Tour premium expected. Weigh prestige vs wallet hit.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Turkey blasts onto the scene fusing Bosphorus beauty with Tour de France pedigree—106km of paved punishment through Beykoz's hidden gems. Traffic-free pro treatment elevates amateurs to heroes amid 3K global riders. Prestige punches way above untested climbs; Istanbul's allure seals the deal for adventure-starved roadies. Minor unknowns like vert pale against the hype.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Debut dazzler with global glow—pin it for 2026. Tour branding guarantees polish; Istanbul etches memories. Skip if vert-hungry.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour prestige meets Turkish splendor in a 106km stunner.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if craving exotic road prestige sans Alps agony. Skip if chasing 4K+ vert or gravel grit.
final_verdict.alternatives: L'Etape Cyprus for island vert, main Etape du Tour for mythic cols, local Turkish fondos for value.

logistics.airport: Istanbul (IST) 45km north, Sabiha Gökçen (SAW) 40km east—taxis/Ubers swarm, shuttles likely. Fly international, crash Beykoz night before.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Beykoz hotels or Istanbul core—Airbnbs near Küçüksu Pavilion prime. Book early for 3K influx.
logistics.food: Turkish meze feasts pre-race; post-ride kebabs and çay. Vegan options abound in coastal villages.

history.founder: Tour de France organizers with 78 Event
history.origin_story: Born 2024 as L'Etape series hits Turkey via 3-year deal, announced June 4 at Istanbul's Sepetçiler Pavilion. Tour bosses eyed Bosphorus beauty for amateurs craving pro vibes. First edition Oct 19-20 drew 3K from 30 nations, cementing Turkey as 23rd host amid federation cheers for cycling boom.
history.reputation: Instant Tour cred builds buzz; pro safety wows amateurs. Tourism hook positions as global gem. Early rep: polished newcomer.

course_description.character: Beykoz loop unleashes Istanbul's wild side—paved climbs snake through forests, villages, and pavilions. Traffic-free bliss lets you smash king-of-mountain segments amid Polonezkoy charm. 106km blends endurance grind with touristic euphoria, Tour-style without the jet lag.

suffering_zones.label: King of the Mountain
suffering_zones.desc: Steep Beykoz ascent shreds legs early, hairpin hell tests pacing. Crowds fuel the fire as you eye polka dots. Pure amateur GC battleground.

suffering_zones.label: Riva Rollers
suffering_zones.desc: Coastal punchers after village sprints drain reserves. Wind-whipped undulations demand constant power. Mental breaker before finale.

course_description.signature_challenge: Beykoz's cumulative climbs rack 1600m+ over 66 miles, blending relentless gradients with Istanbul panoramas. No single monster but endless testing forges Tour grit. Traffic-free freedom amplifies the hurt.

guide_variables.race_name: L'Etape Turkey by Tour de France

non_negotiables.why: 106km demands steady power for endless Beykoz gradients. Istanbul heat tests hydration; simulate loops. Tour prestige requires finish-line legs.
Mimic traffic-free pace with vert. Builds mental armor for KOMs. Ensures sub-cutoff glory.

citations.title: L'Etape Türkiye by Tour de France: Spectacular cycling journey
